(born 2 August 1988) is a former Indian [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player, from [Calicut](/wiki/Calicut "Calicut"), [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"), who represented India in several international tournaments. He partnered with [Aparna Balan](/wiki/Aparna_Balan "Aparna Balan") and [Alwin Francis](/wiki/Alwin_Francis "Alwin Francis") in mixed doubles category and men's doubles category respectively. His career best world ranking was 37 and 41 in men's doubles and mixed doubles category respectively. Since 2016 he is coach of Indian National Badminton Team.

Indian badminton player (born 1988)

| Tournaments played | [World Badminton Championships](/wiki/World_Badminton_Championships "World Badminton Championships") 2015, 2014, 2013 & 2009  [Asian Games 2010](/wiki/Asian_Games_2010 "Asian Games 2010")  [Asian Badminton Championships](/wiki/Asian_Badminton_Championships "Asian Badminton Championships") 2016, 2015, 2013, 2012, 2011 & 2010  [Sudirman Cup](/wiki/Sudirman_Cup "Sudirman Cup") 2015, 2013, 2011 & 2009   [Thomas Cup](/wiki/Thomas_Cup "Thomas Cup") 2014 |

**Arun Vishnu** (born 2 August 1988) is a former Indian [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player, from [Calicut](/wiki/Calicut "Calicut"), [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"), who represented India in several international tournaments. He partnered with [Aparna Balan](/wiki/Aparna_Balan "Aparna Balan") and [Alwin Francis](/wiki/Alwin_Francis "Alwin Francis") in mixed doubles category and men's doubles category respectively. His career best world ranking was 37 and 41 in men's doubles and mixed doubles category respectively. Since 2016 he is coach of Indian National Badminton Team.

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arun_Vishnu&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Playing career")]

Vishnu started playing badminton at the age of 12 under Kerala Sports Council Coach A. Nazer. He trained at [Gopichand Badminton Academy](/wiki/Gopichand_Badminton_Academy "Gopichand Badminton Academy") in Hyderabad under the guidance of [Pullela Gopichand](/wiki/Pullela_Gopichand "Pullela Gopichand").[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

Vishnu and [Aparna Balan](/wiki/Aparna_Balan "Aparna Balan") were [Indian National Badminton Champion](/wiki/List_of_Indian_National_Badminton_champions "List of Indian National Badminton champions") in Mixed Doubles category in 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014 & 2015. He and [Tarun Kona](/wiki/Tarun_Kona "Tarun Kona") were [Indian National Badminton Champion](/wiki/List_of_Indian_National_Badminton_champions "List of Indian National Badminton champions") in Men's Doubles category in 2011.

Vishnu and [Alwin Francis](/wiki/Alwin_Francis "Alwin Francis") won Men Doubles Gold medal at National Games 2011, Ranchi. Vishnu and [Aparna Balan](/wiki/Aparna_Balan "Aparna Balan") won Mixed Doubles Gold Medal at National Games 2015, Kochi.

He has represented India in [World Badminton Championships](/wiki/World_Badminton_Championships "World Badminton Championships") 2015, 2014, 2013 & 2009, [Asian Games 2010](/wiki/Asian_Games_2010 "Asian Games 2010"), Asian Badminton Championships 2016, 2015, 2013, 2012, 2011 & 2010, [Sudirman Cup](/wiki/Sudirman_Cup "Sudirman Cup") 2015, 2013, 2011 & 2009, and [Thomas Cup](/wiki/Thomas_Cup "Thomas Cup") 2014. In the [Indian Badminton League](/wiki/Indian_Badminton_League "Indian Badminton League") 2013, Vishnu played for the [Pune Pistons](/wiki/Pune_Pistons "Pune Pistons").

## Coaching career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arun_Vishnu&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Coaching career")]

Vishnu started coaching the Indian National Badminton Team from 2016. He was the coach of Indian Badminton Team for the following major tournaments:
[Commonwealth Games 2022](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games_2022 "Commonwealth Games 2022"), Asian Games 2018 & 2022, World Badminton Championship 2021, 2022 & 2023,
Thomas & Uber Cup 2021, Badminton Asia Championship 2022 & 2023, Badminton Asia Team Championship 2024, Badminton Asia Mixed Team Championship 2023, [All England Championship](/wiki/All_England_Championship "All England Championship") 2022, 2023 & 2024, Junior World Badminton Championship 2017 & 2018.

As a Coach, Vishnu guided [Chennai Smashers](/wiki/Chennai_Smashers "Chennai Smashers") to [Premier Badminton League](/wiki/Premier_Badminton_League "Premier Badminton League") 2017 title on his maiden assignment as Coach.

Currently he trains[Treesa Jolly](/wiki/Treesa_Jolly "Treesa Jolly") & [Gayatri Gopichand](/wiki/Gayatri_Gopichand "Gayatri Gopichand"), [Tanisha Crasto](/wiki/Tanisha_Crasto "Tanisha Crasto") & [Ashwini Ponnappa](/wiki/Ashwini_Ponnappa "Ashwini Ponnappa") in Indian National Badminton Camp along with [Pullela Gopichand](/wiki/Pullela_Gopichand "Pullela Gopichand")

## Achievements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arun_Vishnu&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Achievements")]

### BWF Grand Prix

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arun_Vishnu&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: BWF Grand Prix")]

The BWF Grand Prix had two levels, the [BWF Grand Prix and Grand Prix Gold](/wiki/BWF_Grand_Prix_and_Grand_Prix_Gold "BWF Grand Prix and Grand Prix Gold"). It was a series of badminton tournaments sanctioned by the [Badminton World Federation](/wiki/Badminton_World_Federation "Badminton World Federation") (BWF) which was held from 2007 to 2017.

## Family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arun_Vishnu&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Family")]

In 2016 January, Arun Vishnu married [Nagpur](/wiki/Nagpur "Nagpur") native badminton player [Arundhati Pantawane](/wiki/Arundhati_Pantawane "Arundhati Pantawane").[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) The couple has a son, Adharv Arun Vishnu.

## References
